programme.desc: Sister Steve puts herself in danger by swapping places with a British aristocrat to infiltrate a trade delegation, leaving Frank with his hands full trying to protect her. Guest starring David McCallum (NCIS), with Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: The crime-busting cleric and Sister Steve investigate a murder at a bustling city hospital. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A rookie officer goes undercover as a drug dealer in an attempt to keep heroin out of schools, but his assignment becomes complicated when he meets an old friend

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A woman seemingly dies as the result of a shark attack, but inquiries prove she had already been shot. As the investigation proceeds, it transpires the victim worked for a private investigation agency that specialised in entrapping married men suspected of infidelity - and a member of the CSI team becomes a leading suspect in her death. David Caruso stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the private life of a murdered Navy captain whose actions may have led to his death, while McGee finds his ideal woman on the internet - but it turns out she is too good to be true. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray, Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason defends a client accused of murder when the duelling scene in a production of 'Romeo and Juliet' takes on sudden reality

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is discovered during a city-wide power cut - and because Jethro and the team are unable to use their modern electronic equipment, they have to solve the case the old-fashioned way, and it soon becomes apparent that national security could be at stake. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A Marine Corps officer vows to avenge his sister's murder, and Walker must find him before another life is lost. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. Meanwhile, crisis counsellor Miles Wolf carries out psychological assessments of all the agents in the wake of the bombings. Guest starring Steve Valentine (Crossing Jordan)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate a government organisation dedicated to child prodigies while researching the death of a marine whose body appeared in the middle of a field disguised as a scarecrow, and realise a 12-year-old could be the killer's next target. programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan is arrested for the murder of Gordon Ganza, a Mob boss suspected of ordering an attempt on Steve's life. Feature-length episode, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Steve races to prove his father's innocence by finding the real culprit in the Ganza murder, convinced Malcolm Trainor is the man responsible for the crime. Medical detective drama, starring Barry and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A nursing administrator is found dead during a hospital emergency drill - and an eccentric artist proves to be the only witness. Medical detective drama, guest starring Elliott Gould, Loretta Swit, Sally Kellerman, William Christopher and Jamie Farr

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is asked to investigate when three convicts break out of prison, including a brilliant hacker who was forced to help the other criminals steal billions of dollars online. Meanwhile, Paige struggles to manage Ralph's expectations when his father returns. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A musical prodigy assists Scorpion in investigating the murder of his friend, who was a music industry blogger. Meanwhile, Drew takes Ralph to a baseball game and later visits the headquarters to seek Walter's advice in a bid to help him connect with his son. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. A phone call from the man himself makes it clear he is out for revenge, and Sam Ryan has information as to the motive behind it. Guest starring Richard Schiff (The West Wing)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison receives a message from a woman whose spouse has murdered and decapitated her. The husband is caught and the case goes to trial, but then something unexpected happens. Meanwhile, Joe skips his therapy session and becomes increasingly distant. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A man holds an antenatal class hostage, and the expectant mothers all start going into labour. Fortunately, Dr Sloan is on hand to resolve matters. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Three talk-show hosts housed in the same studio devise bizarre and outlandish pranks as they vie for ratings, but the tragic result of one joke turns out to be no laughing matter. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed in what initially seems to be a road-rage incident - until investigations reveal evidence of foul play. Digging deeper, the team finds the dead man belonged to an underground fight club, and sets about discovering whether his involvement was a factor in the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, the team works overtime to solve a robbery - a case made doubly difficult by the fact that the prime suspect is a former petty officer who has supposedly been dead for 17 years. Eventually arrested at a Vietnam memorial, he manages to convince Gibbs of his innocence - but a discovery in the forensics lab suggests he may be guilty after all. Guest starring Peter Coyote

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee is sent to a women's prison to interview a murder suspect, but ends up being captured by the inmates, who have run riot and seized control of a cell block, afraid they will be blamed for killing a guard. Held hostage as a bargaining chip, he tries to identify the real culprit before the warden takes drastic action to resolve the crisis

programme.desc: Ducky is attacked by a knife-wielding woman who accuses him of war crimes, alleging he tortured and killed her brother in Afghanistan in the early 1980s. What none of them realise is that Tommy is back in Hebden Bridge, weakened by his injuries and hiding out in an abandoned narrowboat, where he starts plotting to see his son. Sarah Lancashire, Siobhan Finneran, James Norton and Karl Davies star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. A massive police search leads to the body of a missing five-year-old, the victim of sexual assault and strangulation. All the evidence points to a local drunkard being the guilty party _ but as the case goes to trial it becomes clear that the outcome is by no means assured. Gripping crime drama by Lynda La Plante, starring David Hayman, Kate Buffery, Helen McCrory and Rhys Ifans

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer acquires a broken clock that holds the key to the honour of a family and a regiment thanks to some love letters from the Napoleonic era discovered inside. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Lovejoy

However, as soon as the roguish dealer arrives, he becomes embroiled in a murder. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Part two of two. While in Venice investigating his friend's death, Lovejoy discovers that not only is the beautiful Caterina saving art for herself rather than posterity, but so too is her sister Lavinia. Fulton Mackay, Gwen Humble and Haydn Gwynne guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Despatched by Munro to kill a French colonel, Sharpe is wounded in battle and hovers at death's door - but Harper and a mute nun nurse him back to health. Once recovered, he sets out in search of vengeance with a newly forged sword. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean and Daragh O'Malley

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Green-fingered amateur detectives Laura and Rosemary are called in to tackle a stubborn species of weed threatening an entire year's vintage at the Crickle Valley vineyard. However, they find themselves in the right place at the right time when the body of a wine critic is discovered on the premises. Murder mystery, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The amateur sleuths are called to a monastery to restore a herb garden in time for a royal visit - but their pruning and planting is disrupted by the discovery of a corpse. As they investigate the death, the tight-knit community closes in around them. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo help reorganise the seed museum owned by the descendants of famous botanist Edwin Pargeter - but it is not long before a man is found dead in the family's garden. In an effort to trace his killer, Rosemary involves colleagues from the Royal Horticultural Society at Kew Gardens, and soon the race is on to identify a batch of mysterious seeds before it falls into the wrong hands. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are invited to help create an Arabian-style garden in Malaga. Before long, a local resident is found dead, and a mysterious landowner is the chief suspect. When it transpires that someone has blocked the mountainside channels on which the new garden will rely for its supply of water, it appears the precious commodity may provide a motive for the killing. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd is called upon to investigate when a rugby coach's body is found stripped naked and tied to a goal post. His team's losing streak of 50 straight games has broken national records, and Shepherd is perplexed by a sense of relief in the community. Starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: When an amnesiac wanders into their lives, Frank Hathaway and Luella Shakespeare must work out what happened to him. Comedy drama, starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The wife of prominent charity campaigner Gregor Jack is murdered, and Rebus wonders whether her husband committed the crime - especially as he was recently spotted patronising a brothel. Jack's PA and publicist seem to have secrets to hide, but when evidence linking the victim to a convicted sex offender also comes to light, the detective must figure out which of his suspects is really responsible. Ken Stott stars

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective investigates the meticulously planned murder of an elderly birdwatcher, and begins to suspect that he is on the trail of a vengeful serial killer. However, the case is jeopardised when he becomes too close to one of the key suspects. Drama, starring Kenneth Branagh, Sarah Smart, David Warner, Tom Hiddleston, Saskia Reeves and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A black man is murdered in a jail cell by a confirmed racist - a crime the prison service blames on overcrowding. However, the judge's probing reveals the institution wasn't operating at full capacity. A drink-driving appeal puts the bench at loggerheads, and Jo distances herself from her former lover. With Donald Sinden and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Charlie is appointed to defend an animal rights activist accused of murdering an eminent scientist - a case that will be heard before her father. The defence barrister's support for her client's cause leads her to make a series of unprofessional outbursts in court, while Jo's relationship with Marc Thompson creates tension

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Fantasy drama, starring John Simm as a detective who ends up in a coma while pursuing a serial killer - and when he comes round, he finds he has somehow been transported back to 1973. As he attempts to uncover what has happened to him, he must also contend with the macho behaviour of his Sweeneyesque colleagues, as well as a murderer whose crimes are oddly similar to the one he was investigating in his own time

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate's day begins in high spirits with a driving assessment supervised by Tony, but events escalate when he fails to keep up with a stolen car, which is later involved in a hit-and-run. The investigations lead to the driver, but also reveal the victim's family has been under intolerable strain, and her brother has been making ends meet by dabbling in motor theft and drug-running

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A teenage runaway is found dead from an overdose near a known drug users' haunt and fingerprints on the syringe suggest she was murdered. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A rare disease spreads around Poplar Street, and Patsy and Dr Turner try to trace the outbreak to its source. Suspicion over the identity of the first carrier threatens to divide a previously close-knit family. Sister Winifred is forced to confront her prejudices while treating a patient, and Barbara worries about how Trixie will react when she agrees to go to dinner with Tom

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is seconded to a maternity ward, and is impressed by how modern technology is being used to help the mothers - but is left with a moral dilemma after assisting on a traumatic birth. A young man from Poplar is delighted to be accepted into university, but his mother is less than happy. His dreams are jeopardised when his girlfriend gets pregnant, a development that puts pressure on the whole family

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Rag Nymph
programme.desc: Feature-length period melodrama set in Victorian times, charting the relationship between feisty Newcastle rag-woman Aggie Winkowski and 10-year-old orphan Millie Forester. Determined to protect Millie from the seamy world of prostitution, Aggie resolves to take the youngster under her wing. Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic, fearing they were being abused or neglected. Her husband admits that she has been behaving strangely lately, and when she is referred to a neurologist by Dr Turner, the truth proves devastating for the whole family. Christopher admits that Alexandra has been unhappy since his divorce, and Violet and Fred organise a beauty contest to raise money for charity. Valerie and Magda both sign up and quickly become close friends - leading to the sharing of a terrible secret

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The shrewd spinster is sent to convalesce in the tropical paradise of Barbados, where she finds life decidedly dull - until a grisly double murder breaks the monotony and presents her with an unforeseen opportunity to do some serious sleuthing. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Donald Pleasence and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' daring and controversial adaptation of Sarah Waters' novel of lesbian love set in the 1890s. The three-part drama begins as Nan Astley realises she is attracted to other women and embarks on a series of adventures, which take her from her parents' oyster parlour in Whitstable to the glamour of London. Starring Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Realising she cannot return to her family life in Whitstable, Nan attempts to earn money by posing as a male prostitute on the streets of London, disguising herself with the aid of stage costumes. It isn't long before she meets a wealthy society widow who offers her a better life - at a perilous price. Rachael Stirling, Keeley Hawes and Anna Chancellor star

programme.title: Tipping the Velvet
programme.desc: Part three of three. Thrown out by Diana and left destitute by Blake, Nan turns to Florence for help. At first her arrival seems unwelcome, as the idealistic charity worker has settled into a life of domesticity - but the chilly reception soon turns out to be a charade and before long the pair have rekindled their friendship

Tens of people were wrongly prosecuted over a period of 15 years and more, with some incarcerated, thanks to a flawed Royal Mail computer system. Here, Dot Davies meets two victims and contemplates who should be held liable for a scandal that has torn lives apart

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a tour across the UK looking at some of the most memorable glass items bought by competing teams, before seeing how they fared at auction. He also tries his hand at glass cutting and shares his tips when it comes to buying and selling glass. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp blows some glass and Roo Irvine discusses her passion for glass with Charlie Ross

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Slovakia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second Group E match for both sides, which comes from Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only previously played each other five times, and this is their first meeting at a major tournament finals. Both teams got off to encouraging starts, with Sweden holding Spain to a hard-fought goalless draw despite seeing little of the ball, while the Slovakians earned a 2-1 victory over Poland, with Inter Milan defender Milan Skriniar scoring the winner. Analysis comes from Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Ashley Williams, while Robyn Cowen is the commentator

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards discuss the best goals ever scored at the European Championships

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Croatia v Czech Republic (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Group D fixture, which takes place at Hampden Park. Both Gareth Southgate and Steve Clarke will be keeping a close eye on this game, as the result here could have major ramifications for England and Scotland's qualification hopes, with the Czechs looking to build on their victory over Scotland, and Croatia aiming to register their first point after losing to England. The most recent game between the sides - and only the third in history - came during the group stages of Euro 2016 in France. Crowd trouble and flares thrown onto the pitch marred the occasion, which ended in a 2-2 draw in Saint-Etienne. Ivan Perisic and Ivan Rakitic had given Croatia a 2-0 lead, but the Czechs hit back through Milan Skoda's header, before Tomas Necid smashed in a penalty in the 93rd minute to snatch a point. Analysis comes from Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann, with Steve Bower and Dion Dublin on commentary

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby City's Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne delve in to at all the big Euro 2020 matches, including reaction to Scotland v England. They are joined by guests Harry Redknapp, Anne-Marie, Jordan North and Mo Gilligan for some lively banter and there's footage of Crouchy's time spent with some of the Home Nations players before they headed tournament

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Scotland and Croatia v Czech Republic in Group D, and Sweden v Slovakia in Group E. The last time the auld enemies met at a major tournament, Paul Gascoigne scored a famous volley as England went on to make the semi-finals of Euro 96 and Scotland narrowly exited the group stage on goals scored. Scotland's Wembley victory in 1999 is their only away win in this fixture in 40 years. With analysis from Micah Richards and Charlie Adam

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Scotland. A chance to see the Group D fixture between the old rivals, which came from Wembley Stadium. England had won eight of the 11 most recent meetings, including a famous victory on home soil at Euro 96, while Scotland were bidding for only a second away triumph in this fixture since 1981. With commentary from Guy Mowbray, Martin Keown and James McFadden

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Tom Parker Bowles, and special guest Angela Scanlon. Olly Smith joins via video link and picks wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of a six-bedroom house full of period features in Newport, south Wales, a two-bedroom flat near Blackpool's Golden Mile with lots of potential and a mid-terrace house in Bradford

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Hungary v France (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' second Group F fixture, which takes place at Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Hungarians put up a good fight in their opener against holders Portugal and were level until Raphael Guerreiro's 84th-minute opener, but a late brace from Cristiano Ronaldo ensured the match resulted in a perhaps harsh 3-0 scoreline. The French are bidding to go one better than when they were runners-up in 2016 and got off to a good start with a 1-0 win over Germany, with a Mats Hummels own goal dividing the sides. Analysis is provided by Alan Shearer and Alex Scott, while commentary comes from Jonathan Pearce and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are dancer and percussionist Bez, actress Maya Sondhi, former professional footballer Darren Bent, and florist and entrepreneur Elizabeth McKenna, who answer questions on beekeeping, Julie Walters, James Bond films and the life and works of Watson Fothergill

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Poland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Group E fixture, which takes place at Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. This is only the 11th meeting between these countries, and Spain hold a firm advantage in their previous matches, winning eight and losing only once, with one draw. Their most recent encounter came back in June 2010, when Spain recorded a thumping 6-0 win in Murcia, a matter of weeks before they won the World Cup in South Africa. However, the Spaniards were not firing on all cylinders in their opener against Sweden, when they could only manage a goalless draw. Poland will also be disappointed with their opening match, having lost 2-1 to Slovakia, with their cause not helped by Grzegorz Krychowiak's red card. Rio Ferdinand, Jurgen Klinsmann and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy on commentary

programme.title: Meet Joe Black (1998)
programme.desc: Death takes over the body of a recently deceased young man, and embarks on a mission to claim the life of a powerful media tycoon, but ends up falling in love with his daughter instead - leaving him on the horns of a dilemma. Remake of 1934 romantic fantasy Death Takes a Holiday, starring Brad Pitt, Anthony Hopkins and Claire Forlani

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Portugal v Germany. A chance to see the second Group F fixture for both sides, which took place at Fußball Arena München. With commentary from Simon Brotherton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher presents topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of the singles final on day seven of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club. Feliciano Lopez won this title when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Gilles Simon in 6-2, 6-7, 7-6. Despite receiving a second-round walkover against the injured Juan Martin del Potro, Lopez very much did things the hard way, with all of his matches going to a deciding set

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Mark receives word that his father has passed away, and he is given the chance to leave prison for a day to attend the funeral. However, at the last moment, contraband belonging to Daniel is discovered in their cell, and his temporary release is cancelled. He later gets given the chance to take part in a crime and punishment conference, an opportunity Jackson seeks to exploit. Eric is forced to take greater risks to protect his family - but finally has to face the consequences of his actions

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final matches in Group A - Wales v Italy at Stadio Olimpico in Rome and Switzerland v Turkey at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales' triumph against Russia in their concluding group game in 2016 ensured they made it to the knockout stage of a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Today they faced four-time world champions Italy, who have reached at least the quarter-finals of this championship four times out of five this century

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from recent series of the motoring show. Featuring another outing for Paddy McGuinness's ice cream van, a tribute to Stirling Moss, the Top Gear Mid Life Crisis Triathlon, and two very different takes on Italian Supercar

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Wales. A chance to see the final Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. With commentary from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh visits a Norfolk house where someone had already done half the renovation work before selling it at the auction. Dion Dublin is in Leicestershire, and playing detective to find out where some drips are coming from, while Martin Roberts has fun wielding a big stick at a dated dwelling in Derbyshire

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Jason, Mark, Kiran and Angela vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Don Warrington, Zoe Lyons, Hugh Dennis and Giovanna Fletcher, who discuss Agent Running in the Field by John le Carré and Another Life by Jodie Chapman, and also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Crewe, a town steeped in railway history, to investigate the making of cinema classic The Night Mail during the 1930s. He then heads to Chester to discover the interwar origins of Britain's most popular zoo, then goes across the border to the Welsh village of Gresford, scene of one of Britain's worst mining disasters

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day five of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the singles quarter-finals. The corresponding stage of the tournament staged here in 2019 produced an upset to delight the crowd, with wildcard entrant Feliciano Lopez securing a 4-6, 6-4, 7-6 triumph over sixth seed Milos Raonic. Elsewhere, their were also victories for Felix Auger-Aliassime, Daniil Medvedev, and Gilles Simon over Stefanos Tsitsipas, Diego Schwartzman, and Nicolas Mahut respectively

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied from the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a journey through Germany, steered by a Bradshaw's guide. His unique window on Europe between the wars takes him through a dark period in German history, when the nation's first democracy and its vibrant culture of art, design and decadence were swept away by fascism, nationalism and the increasing likelihood of war

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest

programme.title: Inside Culture
programme.desc: In a special programme, Mary Beard is in conversation with critically acclaimed and internationally bestselling Nigerian author Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie. Her latest book, Notes on Grief, conveys her startlingly powerful reaction to losing her father last year, the man she credits with instilling in her a strong feminist instinct. Her writing is both tender and fierce, whether it's her Women's Prize-winning novel Half of a Yellow Sun or her influential essay We Should All Be Feminists, and she's often named as one of Africa's best writers. In her first public appearance since the news of her mother's death, she talks to Mary about the power of literature to heal, how it feels to have Beyonce using her words in her songs and why Enid Blyton was her inspiration to write as she was growing up in Nigeria

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As midsummer approaches, it's the perfect time for Monty Don to place succulents outside at Longmeadow, as well as planting out dahlias and tender vegetables. Carol Klein visits Trebah Garden in Cornwall, where she finds plants which can add early summer pazazz to borders. Nick Bailey is at RHS Wisley to look at a variety of shrubs that can be used as alternatives to traditional box hedging

programme.title: A Pandemic Poem: Where Did the World Go?
programme.desc: An examination of the pandemic, using a poem written by Poet Laureate Simon Armitage that charts the progression of Covid-19 from the early rumours arriving from Wuhan to the rollout of the vaccination programme and the easing of lockdown

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools is joined by rapper and songwriter Kano, fresh from his performance at Glastonbury Festival's Live at Worthy Farm Event. He chats to Jools about his latest projects, and chooses his favourite moments from the Later archives. There are also debut appearances on the show by Self Esteem and Joel Culpepper

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Spotlight (2015)
programme.desc: A team of investigative reporters for a Boston newspaper look into claims of children being molested within the Catholic church. The journalists discover the situation is far worse than it seemed, as they find a conspiracy among the city's legal, religious and government establishments to cover up decades of abuse. Oscar-winning fact-based drama, starring Mark Ruffalo, Michael Keaton, Rachel McAdams and Stanley Tucci

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part one of three. The search continues for Hera, who is being held by Cavil and the Cylon rebels, and the fleet's members are forced to choose between taking the mission or staying behind in relative safety. With Galactica nearing the end of its life, Adama leads the charge, accompanied by a faithful handful of followers who believe Hera is their only hope for the future

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of three. With Earth, once hoped to be a refuge for the survivors of the 12 colonies, revealed as a nuclear wasteland, and the deterioration of the Galactica itself, the future of the human race is uncertain. Adama leads a small force of loyalists to rescue half-human-half-Cylon child Hera from the clutches of Cavil and his Cylon faction

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part three of three. Last-ever episode of the epic sci-fi series. Gaius and Caprica have a startling revelation, while Roslin struggles to cope with the sights she has seen as the destiny of the humans and Cylons seems to be more fragile than ever. Starring Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood takes a walk on the wild side, working as a zookeeper at the largest zoo in the UK. As he mucks in, he discovers that the zoo's conservation efforts are helping make sure that many of these endangered species survive in the wild for generations to come

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce a collection of weird clips from the natural world, this time featuring a man who was struck by lightning, an everlasting storm and a shocking secret lurking in the swamps of South America

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Jilan, 12, has a passion for politics and hopes to be prime minister one day, so she wants a den where she can plan her campaigns in peace and quiet

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Myles and Kathryn battle it out and are joined by Blue Peter's Lyndsey Russell and comedians Vikki Stone, Ria Lina and Nathan Caton

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Scientists Maddie Moate and Greg Foot create a huge rainbow in the studio, the cast of Jamie Johnson challenge team BP, and there is music from the cast of The Prince of Egypt

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve's search sees him chomp through a car like a T-rex, wield an axe like an Allosaurus and hitch a ride in Britain's biggest tank to showcase the Spinosaurus's size and power

programme.title: Earth's Seasonal Secrets
programme.desc: With the sun shining and the flowers blooming, summer is a season of splendid abundance, and the long hours of daylight make life burst out in a riot of activity. However, in certain regions there can be sweltering heat. As this documentary reveals, for a range of species, from sneaky ringtailed lemurs to battling ibex and penguins to colour-changing lizards, summer is a time when the living is not always easy. Narrated by Andrew Scott

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters look back on trips across the continent, with Gordon Buchanan reflecting on a year in the forests of Minnesota getting to know a family of wild black bears. Chris Packham recalls a magical moment when a sperm whale popped up alongside his boat, as well as the time he got a little too close for comfort to a grey whale's blowhole, while Liz Bonnin recounts the time she filmed alligators in Florida during their breeding season

programme.title: Letter from an Unknown Woman (1948)
programme.desc: A teenage girl becomes infatuated with a renowned concert pianist. As she grows older, her yearning for him develops into a deep and obsessive love. However, he only discovers the truth about her feelings when it is too late. Romantic drama directed by Max Ophuls, starring Joan Fontaine, Louis Jourdan, Marcel Journet, Mady Christians and Howard Freeman

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An insight into the life of one of Hollywood's best-loved actresses, telling the story of her Oscar-winning career by using rarely seen archive footage of her appearances on the BBC

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day six of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring the singles semi-finals. Feliciano Lopez and Gilles Simon both came from a set down to defeat seeded players at this stage when the tournament was last held in 2019, overcoming Felix Auger-Aliassime and Daniil Medvedev respectively

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from a Gloucester as Catherine Southon and Michael Baggott select the best antiques and collectables to take to auction

programme.title: The Great Northern Garden Build
programme.desc: Documentary following the creation of RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford, one of the most ambitious and challenging gardening projects the UK has ever seen. With the major construction work well under way, what impact will a global pandemic have on the build, and will the garden be ready in time for its grand opening?

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of the Australian island state's wildlife. The programme reveals a surprisingly gentle side to the island's most famous resident, the Tasmanian devil, and also features footage of rare white wallabies, venomous jack jumper ants, and caves illuminated by thousands of glow worms. Tasmania's rivers are home to platypus and giant lobsters, while on the coast, miniature penguins come ashore to breed

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Contributors including Bonnie Tyler, Tracie Young, Marilyn and members of New Order, Kajagoogoo, Haysi Fantayzee, Spandau Ballet, the Smiths, Flying Pickets and Thompson Twins reflect on an era when the mood in the charts was escapism and musical reinvention. It was a difficult time for the Top of the Pops itself, as the rise of the video started to threaten its unique appeal - getting the day's top artists into the studio. This forced producer Michael Hurll to experiment with presenters and features - including dispensing with dance troupe Zoo

programme.title: Paul Weller Live at the Barbican
programme.desc: Edith Bowman presents the singer-songwriter's concert at the London venue in May, his first live gig in two years, when he was backed by the BBC Symphony Orchestra, with arrangements by conductor Jules Buckley. It features songs drawn from Weller's long career, with classics from the Jam, the Style Council and his four decades as a solo artist, with guest appearances by Boy George, James Morrison and Celeste

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis pan through the last week and extract nuggets of comedy gold, with guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Glenn Moore and Evelyn Mok

programme.title: Weller at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of performances tracking the career of singer-songwriter Paul Weller, with footage spanning his career revealing how he evolved from his beginnings in mod-punk trio the Jam to the more soulful sound of the Style Council and finally on to his solo work The programme also features Weller duetting with Noel Gallagher on That's Entertainment, and with Amy Winehouse on Etta Jones' Don't Go to Strangers

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A compilation of some of the biggest hits of the year, celebrating the genres of soul, reggae, jazz, new wave and pop. Wham!, KC and the Sunshine Band, the Police, Culture Club, Siouxsie and the Banshees, UB40, Duran Duran, the Beat and Bananarama feature, alongside Elton John, Bonnie Tyler and Malcolm McLaren's Double Dutch

programme.title: Out of Blue (2018)
programme.desc: Mike Hoolihan is a New Orleans cop investigating the murder of astrophysicist Jennifer Rockwell, a black hole expert found shot dead in her observatory. As Mike delves deeper into the case, she finds herself coping with questions of quantum mechanics, parallel universes, and exploding stars. Director Carol Morley's drama based on the Martin Amis novel, starring Patricia Clarkson, Toby Jones and James Caan

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: The Gardeners' World presenter's year-long diary about the life cycle of her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon. In summer, Carol deadheads the roses and stakes plants to help keep the plot looking its best and savours the full blooms of geraniums and lilies. She creates colourful borders using cannas, ginger and dahlias, before visiting her local beach, Braunton Burrows, to seek out sea holly, which she hopes to grow herself

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Gardeners in Goring-on-Thames, Oxfordshire, aim to transform the train station, brighten up the pathway to the high street and fill the village with hanging baskets

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As midsummer approaches, it's the perfect time for Monty Don to place succulents outside at Longmeadow, as well as planting out dahlias and tender vegetables. Carol Klein visits Trebah Garden in Cornwall, where she finds plants which can add early summer pazazz to borders. Nick Bailey is at RHS Wisley to look at a variety of shrubs that can be used as alternatives to traditional box hedging

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani head to Co Down to catch up with the dairy farming Lilburn family. Elsewhere, Adam Henson discovers how science is helping to save rare breeds, Charlotte Smith investigates a shadow pandemic of rural domestic violence, and John Craven launches the 30th Countryfile Photographic Competition

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is in Sutherland to see why Forestry & Land Scotland (formerly the Forestry Commission) is pulling up trees and restoring peatland. Euan McIllwraith meets the author of the Scots Dictionary of Nature, the sparks are flying as JJ Chalmers tries a bit of blacksmithing, and Anne Lundon puts on her bee suit to visit the hives of a 'bee whisperer'

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson is back in her Inverleith Park allotment, where she is setting up a wormery and looking forward to a pretty and productive harvest. Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray show how to prune and tame overgrown evergreens, and the Beechgrowers return with further updates in their gardening video diaries from the length and breadth of Scotland

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Double Dynamite (1951)
programme.desc: A penniless bank teller comes to the aid of a bookie, who returns the favour by placing a bet on his behalf. The good Samaritan ends up a wealthy man as a result, but when his workplace is robbed, he has a hard time proving to the police where his newfound riches came from. Comedy, starring Frank Sinatra, Jane Russell and Groucho Marx

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones celebrates Father's Day with a look at inspirational dads, with award-winning rap artist and author Guvna B chatting to Radzi Chinyanganya

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. Over the years, the family experiences the key events of the nation's history, including the gold rush, the Civil War, and the building of the railroads. Epic Western, starring James Stewart, John Wayne, Gregory Peck, Henry Fonda, Debbie Reynolds, Lee J Cobb and Richard Widmark

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Will Axon assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including a Busby hat and a Worcester coffee set. Presenter Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers face three final assignments, including a wildcard challenge where they are given free rein to try and get one last extraordinary shot for their portfolios. They are also in Glencoe for a dramatic landscape challenge and must curate and mount what they hope will be a winning exhibition for the judges. Choosing a maximum of 15 images from the hundreds they've taken across the course of the series is a huge task in itself

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade explores central Africa, travelling through Gabon, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. He investigates plans to develop a sustainable version of palm oil farming, meets a champion boxer helping to rehabilitate child soldiers, and talks to one of Africa's most outspoken political campaigners. He also reports on how conflict between militia groups influenced by foreign powers lies at the heart of the problems that have made the Democratic Republic of Congo arguably the most chaotic and corrupt country in Africa, as well as discovering how the conflict has impacted on the country's few remaining gorillas

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters look back on trips across the continent, with Steve Backshall reflecting on being dropped into the nest of a wild saltwater crocodile to help scientists in the Northern Territory to assess the species' health. Gordon Buchanan reveals how faced his own fear of sharks when he visited Owarigi in the Solomon Islands, and Liz Bonnin looks back on witnessing the hatching of baby green sea turtles on Heron Island on the Great Barrier Reef,

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: Brian explores the enigma of time, a phenomenon that is taken for granted but which, as far as physicists are concerned, is one of the biggest mysteries in the universe. He recalls highlights from his TV series that touch upon different sides of this physics conundrum, from breaking the sound barrier in a Eurofighter in order to see two sunsets in one day, to witnessing one of the oldest animal life cycles on Earth on a remote beach in Costa Rica

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The explorer travels to the far north of Canada, where he joins members of the Gwich'in tribe as they prepare to head out on their annual spring caribou hunt - and discovers why the tradition has come under threat from oil exploration. He then learns more about the industry's impact on the native way of life when he visits the Alberta tar sands, home to the world's second-largest oil reserves

programme.title: Andy Murray: King of Queen's
programme.desc: A look at the tennis star's success in the Wimbledon warm-up event at Queen's Club in London, featuring footage of some of memorable matches from his five singles title wins. Murray has won a record five singles titles on the immaculate grass courts at Queen's. It was the scene of his first win on the ATP Tour back in 2005, and both of his Wimbledon titles were prefaced with victory at the west London venue, which is one of the most prestigious events in men's tennis and one of the highlights of the British sporting summer. The programme features some of his biggest triumphs, including his emotional victory with Feliciano Lopez in the men's doubles there last year, just a few months after major hip surgery

programme.title: Amundsen (2019)
programme.desc: Film drama telling the life story of explorer Roald Amundsen. Inspired by a gift from his father when he was a child, Roald became fascinated by the polar wildernesses. He led the first expedition to the South Pole in 1911 and the first expedition proven to have reached the North Pole, in a dirigible, in 1926. His achievements, however, came at a cost to both his family and his colleagues. Starring Pal Sverre Hagen and Katherine Waterston. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian finds it hard to keep his cool when Fletch blames him for Evie's disappearance. Sacha and Dom prepare to be interviewed for the clinical lead job. Will Sacha's experience win against Dom's ruthless ambition? Josh is determined to crack a mystery case, hoping to impress Ange and rekindle their relationship

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fathers and Songs: Music for Father's Day
programme.desc: A selection of songs that celebrate fatherhood and probe the emotional complexities of a man's relationship with his son or daughter. Featured artists of past and present include Kid Creole and the Coconuts, Cat Stevens, Emmylou Harris, Paul Simon, Madonna, Peter Gabriel, Mike and the Mechanics, Suggs and the Blockheads, Neil Young, James Brown, Ozzy and Kelly Osbourne and Pigbag

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on February 28 1991, featuring performances by N-Joi, Massive Attack, Jesus Jones, Free, the Clash, the Source featuring Candi Staton, Madonna and the Simpsons

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first aired on March 7 1991, featuring performances by Hale & Pace and the Stonkers, Rocky V featuring Joey B Ellis and Tynetta Hare, Quartz introducing Dina Carroll, Living Colour, Ned's Atomic Dustbin, Xpansions, Roxette, the Clash and the Charlatans

programme.title: Pop Charts Britannia: 60 Years of the Top 10
programme.desc: The evolution of feelings and attitudes toward the British singles chart. Documenting changes from the 1952 NME countdown via Pick and Top of the Pops through to the Radio 1 chart show of 2012. With contributions from chart show presenters David Jacobs and Reggie Yates, music fans Grace Dent and Pete Paphides and veteran musicians Jon Webster and Rob Dickins

programme.title: REM at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances from throughout the band's career, originally recorded for BBC programmes including The Old Grey Whistle Test, Top of the Pops, Parkinson, The Late Show and Later with Jools Holland. Songs featured include Losing My Religion, Orange Crush, Pretty Persuasion and Half a World Away. Singer Michael Stipe and bass player Mike Mills also reflect on the band's split in September 2011

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: Huey visits New York City, home to over a million Puerto Ricans and the place where he first fell in love with the hot sounds of Latin Music. 'El Barrio' or Spanish Harlem became the focal point for the community, with Latin music the soundtrack to their survival. Fusing traditional music with American R'n'B and soul, newly minted Nuyoricans created the '60s dance craze, the Boogaloo. Flying south to Puerto Rico itself Huey sets off on a journey to discover the music that was left behind, hunting down the roots of Salsa in the capital San Juan, and the traditional folk styles of Bomba and Plena

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: A performance by the world renowned Cuatro player, the Latin American guitar made famous in Luis Fonsi's hit song Despacito. The Grammy Award-winning star is joined by family and friends in the mountains of Puerto Rico to present some of his favourite music.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles presents the edition first aired on February 28 1991, featuring performances by N-Joi, Massive Attack, Jesus Jones, Free, the Clash, the Source featuring Candi Staton, Madonna and the Simpsons

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first aired on March 7 1991, featuring performances by Hale & Pace and the Stonkers, Rocky V featuring Joey B Ellis and Tynetta Hare, Quartz introducing Dina Carroll, Living Colour, Ned's Atomic Dustbin, Xpansions, Roxette, the Clash and the Charlatans

programme.title: Pop Charts Britannia: 60 Years of the Top 10
programme.desc: The evolution of feelings and attitudes toward the British singles chart. Documenting changes from the 1952 NME countdown via Pick and Top of the Pops through to the Radio 1 chart show of 2012. With contributions from chart show presenters David Jacobs and Reggie Yates, music fans Grace Dent and Pete Paphides and veteran musicians Jon Webster and Rob Dickins

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Clovelly in Devon, looking at its transformation from fishing harbour to romantic Victorian seaside resort. Thanks to a powerful and pioneering woman, Clovelly found itself at the forefront of the holiday revolution, and even today cars are banned and the village is privately owned, helping to preserve it as a reminder of a bygone age

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny and Josie d'Arby introduce the final of the BBC Cardiff Singer of the World Song Prize 2021 from St David's Hall. With judges Neal Davies, Roberta Alexander and Aidan Lang. Featuring the Welsh National Opera Orchestra under conductor Michael Christie

programme.title: An Impossible Love (2018)
programme.desc: An office worker has an affair with a man from a wealthy bourgeois family in the 1950s, which results in the birth of a daughter. However, she is left to raise the child alone when he refuses to marry someone from a lower social class, and faces a 10-year struggle to persuade him to acknowledge his daughter. Drama based on Christine Angot's novel, starring Virginie Efira and Niels Schneider. In French

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund and Strange think the killer they are investigating has claimed a third victim when another body is found - but Special Branch believes the detectives are targeting the wrong suspect. Meanwhile, an under-pressure Buch discovers some surprising secrets about his predecessor. Crime thriller, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund and Strange head to Sweden, hoping to protect the last surviving member of Raben's former army squad - but their investigation is hampered by interference from senior officers and Special Branch. Meanwhile, Buch is put under pressure to go against his principles and pass stringent new anti-terrorism laws. Crime drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund is convinced something was not right with Moeller's burial, and demands his grave be dug up. Raben realises the preacher lied to him about his acquaintance with Anne Dragsholm, while Buch frantically tries to discover the truth about his predecessor's motivations. In Danish

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby presents the first in a series of performances by BBC Orchestras providing an in-depth exploration of famous pieces of classical music. At Glasgow City Halls, the BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra play Gershwin's Rhapsody in Blue, with piano soloist Marc André Hamelin and conductor Thomas Dausgaard. Featuring contributions from composer Julian Joseph and biographer David Benedict

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton explains the creation and importance of maps, discovering the latest technology being used to improve the cartographer's art and revolutionise mankind's knowledge of the world. On a visit to the oldest known map, etched into a hillside 3,000 years ago, he considers how different cultures have approached map-making over millennia, often as a tool for expansionism and political control

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: An examination of the USSR's alliances during World War Two. Laurence Rees hears evidence of Stalin's secret allegiances in the late 1930s, which demonstrate the importance of the Soviet switch to Britain and America's side after its betrayal by the Nazis at the height of war in 1941. He discovers secrets hidden by Russia for more than 50 years and hears from previously silent KGB officers

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The broadcaster and journalist interviews the TV and film writer about his life and career, covering such topics as his memories of working-class Liverpool, how his struggles with speech drove his love of words and the impact of Catholicism on him. The pair also reflect on his writing achievements, from Brookside and Cracker through to controversial 1994 film Priest and the BBC drama Accused

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.
