programme.desc: Kerry and Tim are excited to see what's in their bags from Vinda's party, but Topsy doesn't want to join in the fun. She feels ill and it turns out she has chicken pox

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Catfish is a popular fish in Thailand and today Pearlina is serving it with sticky rice

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran go on a day trip to the seaside to build sandcastles!

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack tells a story about the day Salty wanted an ice-cube for her drink and ended up having an adventure with a lost penguin

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley meets some sheep dogs, as well as a young gardener at a local gardening project, while JB Gill learns all about tractors, before sharing a new Down on the Farm Spring poem

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Everyone is practising their acts for the big talent show, but the curtain won't go up or down and Bob is feeling nervous about performing in front of everyone. So while Bitz tries to get the curtain working, Bob has to try to overcome his nerves and keep the audience happy with a song. Thankfully Purl is there to help and the show, and curtain, are a huge success

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A group of fireflies accidentally spoil Love Monster's stargazing garden party.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Shuffle Snuffler sniffs its way through the jungle. It is horrified when it smells B.O.T.! Can B.O.T. avoid being sneezed to pieces and complete this mission?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes shoe shopping with his friend

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Osbourne shows the class a new toy, but Timmy has to find a way of letting everyone play with it

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When Katie's plan to make Bella a new dress goes wrong, Mali and Sam get solving to try and make their friend happy again

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: The twins wake up early before their first day at school and remember the times they have shared in the run up to the big day

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon and Evie take Waffle for a walk and bump into Evie's friend Anaya and her mum, AJ. An overenthusiastic Waffle drags Simon through the park, soaking him under a sprinkler

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Mr Hammond has been up all night in the Jungle Den at Safari World looking through his tape collection of animal sounds he recorded on a trip down the mighty Mississippi river in North America

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Walter and Winnie the Water Voles are bored. There’s nothing to do at the Tropical Pond. Walter reckons he can get to the Hidden Lake and back by lunchtime. When he doesn’t return, Winnie is worried and heads off to find him. Jago the Robin and Simon the Stickleback are on hand to help, but they can’t find him anywhere. Where is Walter?

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Grandma comes to look after George and Freddie for the day and helps the boys make some special biscuits for Mum. But there’s extra excitement in store when it starts to snow! Will there be enough to build a snowman? Will Grandma brave the sledge? And what will mum think of those biscuits?

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Theo, Dionne, Francis, Oliver, Minnie and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel’s got some socks, a bib and a wooly hat inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different dressing-themed toys and objects too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide safe, baby-appropriate clothes or dressing-up toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to explore with their baby. Some viewers show how they’ve been getting dressed at home too. The fun song We All Like Getting Dressed features, as does a story about Baby Bear dressing up in different outfits. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Sheba the Snake and Click Clack the Crab take centre stage as Ashley and Aimee explore where fairies and elves live and what they do

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bingo and Bluey can’t agree on one simple question: Can Grannies do the floss dance?

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour blue, from the sea to the sky. We also explore the bigger picture of our blue planet. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour blue.

programme.title: Maddie, the Home and You
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran go on a day trip to the seaside to build sandcastles!

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie and Liam are looking for a large piece of paper to do their next big painting on, but when their parents are too busy in the Post Office to help and Grannie Island is unable to assist, the feisty seven-year-old calls at the Lady Artist's house in the hope she will know where to get one from

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: A search for shade on a hot day leads Sarah and Duck to discover a fascinating tower in the park

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Charlie tries to persuade Lola to clean their bedroom

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Two hoots come to visit the Clangers. Tiny wants Big Hoot to play a duet with her

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka cannot find any stones in the garden, so his friends help him. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Join Cat Deeley to celebrate the one thing that makes every family a family... and that’s love!

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell checks out a three-bedroom semi in Alvaston, Derby, and a basement flat in Blackpool. Dion Dublin meets the developer who bought a Liverpool terrace

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a tour across the UK looking at some of the most memorable glass items bought by competing teams, before seeing how they fared at auction. He also tries his hand at glass cutting and shares his tips when it comes to buying and selling glass. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp blows some glass and Roo Irvine discusses her passion for glass with Charlie Ross

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Slovakia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second Group E match for both sides, which comes from Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only previously played each other five times, and this is their first meeting at a major tournament finals. Both teams got off to encouraging starts, with Sweden holding Spain to a hard-fought goalless draw despite seeing little of the ball, while the Slovakians earned a 2-1 victory over Poland, with Inter Milan defender Milan Skriniar scoring the winner. Analysis comes from Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Ashley Williams, while Robyn Cowen is the commentator

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards discuss the best goals ever scored at the European Championships

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Croatia v Czech Republic (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Group D fixture, which takes place at Hampden Park. Both Gareth Southgate and Steve Clarke will be keeping a close eye on this game, as the result here could have major ramifications for England and Scotland's qualification hopes, with the Czechs looking to build on their victory over Scotland, and Croatia aiming to register their first point after losing to England. The most recent game between the sides - and only the third in history - came during the group stages of Euro 2016 in France. Crowd trouble and flares thrown onto the pitch marred the occasion, which ended in a 2-2 draw in Saint-Etienne. Ivan Perisic and Ivan Rakitic had given Croatia a 2-0 lead, but the Czechs hit back through Milan Skoda's header, before Tomas Necid smashed in a penalty in the 93rd minute to snatch a point. Analysis comes from Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann, with Steve Bower and Dion Dublin on commentary

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby City's Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne delve in to at all the big Euro 2020 matches, including reaction to Scotland v England. They are joined by guests Harry Redknapp, Anne-Marie, Jordan North and Mo Gilligan for some lively banter and there's footage of Crouchy's time spent with some of the Home Nations players before they headed tournament

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Scotland and Croatia v Czech Republic in Group D, and Sweden v Slovakia in Group E. The last time the auld enemies met at a major tournament, Paul Gascoigne scored a famous volley as England went on to make the semi-finals of Euro 96 and Scotland narrowly exited the group stage on goals scored. Scotland's Wembley victory in 1999 is their only away win in this fixture in 40 years. With analysis from Micah Richards and Charlie Adam

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Scotland. A chance to see the Group D fixture between the old rivals, which came from Wembley Stadium. England had won eight of the 11 most recent meetings, including a famous victory on home soil at Euro 96, while Scotland were bidding for only a second away triumph in this fixture since 1981. With commentary from Guy Mowbray, Martin Keown and James McFadden

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Tom Parker Bowles, and special guest Angela Scanlon. Olly Smith joins via video link and picks wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of a six-bedroom house full of period features in Newport, south Wales, a two-bedroom flat near Blackpool's Golden Mile with lots of potential and a mid-terrace house in Bradford

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Hungary v France (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' second Group F fixture, which takes place at Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Hungarians put up a good fight in their opener against holders Portugal and were level until Raphael Guerreiro's 84th-minute opener, but a late brace from Cristiano Ronaldo ensured the match resulted in a perhaps harsh 3-0 scoreline. The French are bidding to go one better than when they were runners-up in 2016 and got off to a good start with a 1-0 win over Germany, with a Mats Hummels own goal dividing the sides. Analysis is provided by Alan Shearer and Alex Scott, while commentary comes from Jonathan Pearce and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are dancer and percussionist Bez, actress Maya Sondhi, former professional footballer Darren Bent, and florist and entrepreneur Elizabeth McKenna, who answer questions on beekeeping, Julie Walters, James Bond films and the life and works of Watson Fothergill

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Poland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Group E fixture, which takes place at Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. This is only the 11th meeting between these countries, and Spain hold a firm advantage in their previous matches, winning eight and losing only once, with one draw. Their most recent encounter came back in June 2010, when Spain recorded a thumping 6-0 win in Murcia, a matter of weeks before they won the World Cup in South Africa. However, the Spaniards were not firing on all cylinders in their opener against Sweden, when they could only manage a goalless draw. Poland will also be disappointed with their opening match, having lost 2-1 to Slovakia, with their cause not helped by Grzegorz Krychowiak's red card. Rio Ferdinand, Jurgen Klinsmann and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy on commentary

programme.title: Meet Joe Black (1998)
programme.desc: Death takes over the body of a recently deceased young man, and embarks on a mission to claim the life of a powerful media tycoon, but ends up falling in love with his daughter instead - leaving him on the horns of a dilemma. Remake of 1934 romantic fantasy Death Takes a Holiday, starring Brad Pitt, Anthony Hopkins and Claire Forlani

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Portugal v Germany. A chance to see the second Group F fixture for both sides, which took place at Fußball Arena München. With commentary from Simon Brotherton

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher presents topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents a double bill from the Newark antiques fair, with two sets of reds and blues being helped by experts Ben Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of the singles final on day seven of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club. Feliciano Lopez won this title when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Gilles Simon in 6-2, 6-7, 7-6. Despite receiving a second-round walkover against the injured Juan Martin del Potro, Lopez very much did things the hard way, with all of his matches going to a deciding set

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Mark receives word that his father has passed away, and he is given the chance to leave prison for a day to attend the funeral. However, at the last moment, contraband belonging to Daniel is discovered in their cell, and his temporary release is cancelled. He later gets given the chance to take part in a crime and punishment conference, an opportunity Jackson seeks to exploit. Eric is forced to take greater risks to protect his family - but finally has to face the consequences of his actions

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Elis James is joined by Danny Gabbidon and Natasha Harding to look back at Wales' final group A match against Italy

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final matches in Group A - Wales v Italy at Stadio Olimpico in Rome and Switzerland v Turkey at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales' triumph against Russia in their concluding group game in 2016 ensured they made it to the knockout stage of a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Today they faced four-time world champions Italy, who have reached at least the quarter-finals of this championship four times out of five this century

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Wales. A chance to see the final Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. With commentary from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The music fans stuck with tickets for concerts abroad that were cancelled due to coronavirus, and the static caravan owners whose holiday havens are headed for the junkyard

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is in Sutherland to see why Forestry & Land Scotland (formerly the Forestry Commission) is pulling up trees and restoring peatland. Euan McIllwraith meets the author of the Scots Dictionary of Nature, the sparks are flying as JJ Chalmers tries a bit of blacksmithing, and Anne Lundon puts on her bee suit to visit the hives of a 'bee whisperer'

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Caitlin and her family wait anxiously as her trial draws to a close, while Ellie is left heartbroken and Jac is devastated when she discovers the truth about Joe. Cameron lures Dylan even further into his vengeful plans for Eve

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Paralympians Hannah Cockcroft and Nathan Maguire, father-and-son presenting duo Martin and Roman Kemp and singer Fleur East and her blogger sister Keshia show off their music knowledge by guessing the songs after hearing a few seconds of each track. The winning pair get the chance to win up to £10,000 for a charity of their choice - but the longer they take, the more they lose from the jackpot. Hosted by Marvin and Rochelle Humes

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Poland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Group E fixture, which takes place at Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. This is only the 11th meeting between these countries, and Spain hold a firm advantage in their previous matches, winning eight and losing only once, with one draw. Their most recent encounter came back in June 2010, when Spain recorded a thumping 6-0 win in Murcia, a matter of weeks before they won the World Cup in South Africa. However, the Spaniards were not firing on all cylinders in their opener against Sweden, when they could only manage a goalless draw. Poland will also be disappointed with their opening match, having lost 2-1 to Slovakia, with their cause not helped by Grzegorz Krychowiak's red card. Rio Ferdinand, Jurgen Klinsmann and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy on commentary

programme.title: Meet Joe Black (1998)
programme.desc: Death takes over the body of a recently deceased young man, and embarks on a mission to claim the life of a powerful media tycoon, but ends up falling in love with his daughter instead - leaving him on the horns of a dilemma. Remake of 1934 romantic fantasy Death Takes a Holiday, starring Brad Pitt, Anthony Hopkins and Claire Forlani

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Portugal v Germany. A chance to see the second Group F fixture for both sides, which took place at Fußball Arena München. With commentary from Simon Brotherton

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Canon Michael McMahon of St Conval's, Linwood, and Rev Maggie McTernan of St Margaret's, Newlands, Glasgow lead this week's Reflections. Music is by Gary Innes

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help the Rev Karen and husband Colin, who have £6,000 to turn their oddly-shaped and weed-filled garden in Romsey into an Italian retreat

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of the singles final on day seven of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club. Feliciano Lopez won this title when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Gilles Simon in 6-2, 6-7, 7-6. Despite receiving a second-round walkover against the injured Juan Martin del Potro, Lopez very much did things the hard way, with all of his matches going to a deciding set

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a pair of ex-pats who are raring to return to their roots in North Yorkshire and have a £700,000 budget to find their ideal rural home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents an archive edition Nature in Art in Gloucestershire, the world's first museum and art gallery dedicated to art inspired by nature. While picking up a few artistic tips, Ellie introduces a selection of stories from the archives that demonstrate how the countryside has been an abiding influence, from early man to modern-day artists braving the extremes to capture a landscape or using wildlife itself to create a picture

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Mark receives word that his father has passed away, and he is given the chance to leave prison for a day to attend the funeral. However, at the last moment, contraband belonging to Daniel is discovered in their cell, and his temporary release is cancelled. He later gets given the chance to take part in a crime and punishment conference, an opportunity Jackson seeks to exploit. Eric is forced to take greater risks to protect his family - but finally has to face the consequences of his actions

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final matches in Group A - Wales v Italy at Stadio Olimpico in Rome and Switzerland v Turkey at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales' triumph against Russia in their concluding group game in 2016 ensured they made it to the knockout stage of a major tournament for the first time since 1958. Today they faced four-time world champions Italy, who have reached at least the quarter-finals of this championship four times out of five this century

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from recent series of the motoring show. Featuring another outing for Paddy McGuinness's ice cream van, a tribute to Stirling Moss, the Top Gear Mid Life Crisis Triathlon, and two very different takes on Italian Supercar

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Italy v Wales. A chance to see the final Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. With commentary from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell checks out a three-bedroom semi in Alvaston, Derby, and a basement flat in Blackpool. Dion Dublin meets the developer who bought a Liverpool terrace

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The music fans stuck with tickets for concerts abroad that were cancelled due to coronavirus, and the static caravan owners whose holiday havens are headed for the junkyard

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a distinctive deer stalker hat was the red flag to cracking a web of stolen bank cards. Plus, how one careless text message put a dangerous stalker behind bars for the longest sentence in a police force's history. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes a tour across the UK looking at some of the most memorable glass items bought by competing teams, before seeing how they fared at auction. He also tries his hand at glass cutting and shares his tips when it comes to buying and selling glass. Plus, Natasha Raskin Sharp blows some glass and Roo Irvine discusses her passion for glass with Charlie Ross

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Sweden v Slovakia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second Group E match for both sides, which comes from Saint Petersburg Stadium. These countries have only previously played each other five times, and this is their first meeting at a major tournament finals. Both teams got off to encouraging starts, with Sweden holding Spain to a hard-fought goalless draw despite seeing little of the ball, while the Slovakians earned a 2-1 victory over Poland, with Inter Milan defender Milan Skriniar scoring the winner. Analysis comes from Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Ashley Williams, while Robyn Cowen is the commentator

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards discuss the best goals ever scored at the European Championships

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Croatia v Czech Republic (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the Group D fixture, which takes place at Hampden Park. Both Gareth Southgate and Steve Clarke will be keeping a close eye on this game, as the result here could have major ramifications for England and Scotland's qualification hopes, with the Czechs looking to build on their victory over Scotland, and Croatia aiming to register their first point after losing to England. The most recent game between the sides - and only the third in history - came during the group stages of Euro 2016 in France. Crowd trouble and flares thrown onto the pitch marred the occasion, which ended in a 2-2 draw in Saint-Etienne. Ivan Perisic and Ivan Rakitic had given Croatia a 2-0 lead, but the Czechs hit back through Milan Skoda's header, before Tomas Necid smashed in a penalty in the 93rd minute to snatch a point. Analysis comes from Rio Ferdinand and Jurgen Klinsmann, with Steve Bower and Dion Dublin on commentary

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Kris Marshall is back as the British detective working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears with the first case when a plantation owner ends up dead during a seance - although with the door firmly closed at the time and all the participants holding hands round the table, who did the deed? The victim's family are convinced it was the work of a ghost said to haunt the plantation, but Humphrey is determined to find a more rational explanation. Meanwhile, Fidel has moved on, leaving Officer Dwayne Myers holding the fort - and while he is struggling to cope, the last thing he needs is the Commissioner stepping in to help. Crime drama, with guest appearances from Holby City's Don Gilet and former Corrie bad girl Natalie Gumede

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne delve in to at all the big Euro 2020 matches, including reaction to Scotland v England. They are joined by guests Harry Redknapp, Anne-Marie, Jordan North and Mo Gilligan for some lively banter and there's footage of Crouchy's time spent with some of the Home Nations players before they headed tournament

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Scotland and Croatia v Czech Republic in Group D, and Sweden v Slovakia in Group E. The last time the auld enemies met at a major tournament, Paul Gascoigne scored a famous volley as England went on to make the semi-finals of Euro 96 and Scotland narrowly exited the group stage on goals scored. Scotland's Wembley victory in 1999 is their only away win in this fixture in 40 years. With analysis from Micah Richards and Charlie Adam

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Scotland. A chance to see the Group D fixture between the old rivals, which came from Wembley Stadium. England had won eight of the 11 most recent meetings, including a famous victory on home soil at Euro 96, while Scotland were bidding for only a second away triumph in this fixture since 1981. With commentary from Guy Mowbray, Martin Keown and James McFadden

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Tom Parker Bowles, and special guest Angela Scanlon. Olly Smith joins via video link and picks wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of a six-bedroom house full of period features in Newport, south Wales, a two-bedroom flat near Blackpool's Golden Mile with lots of potential and a mid-terrace house in Bradford

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Hungary v France (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' second Group F fixture, which takes place at Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Hungarians put up a good fight in their opener against holders Portugal and were level until Raphael Guerreiro's 84th-minute opener, but a late brace from Cristiano Ronaldo ensured the match resulted in a perhaps harsh 3-0 scoreline. The French are bidding to go one better than when they were runners-up in 2016 and got off to a good start with a 1-0 win over Germany, with a Mats Hummels own goal dividing the sides. Analysis is provided by Alan Shearer and Alex Scott, while commentary comes from Jonathan Pearce and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a sensory garden for two autistic girls in Aylesbury, while also providing a garden in Liphook for Anne, Shaun and daughter Joanne

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are dancer and percussionist Bez, actress Maya Sondhi, former professional footballer Darren Bent, and florist and entrepreneur Elizabeth McKenna, who answer questions on beekeeping, Julie Walters, James Bond films and the life and works of Watson Fothergill

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a soap opera edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Davood Ghadami, James Bye, Sharon Maughan, Tina Hobley, Steven Arnold, Julia Haworth, Julie Peasgood and Louis Emerick

programme.desc: Peter MacQueen reads Glasraich Oliver by Vivian French, illustrated by Alison Bartlett and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Following the activities of a 10-week-old polar bear being raised by his mother in the Canadian Arctic. Percy was born during the winter in a den kept up to 40 degrees warmer than outside, where the temperature drops to minus 60C and it is dark for months. Polar bears have thick coats, a 10cm thick layer of blubber that acts like thermal underwear, and huge fur-covered paws that are like snowshoes

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod returns home to the island of Lewis and takes a walk up Suainebhal - the hill on Uig where he first discovered his love of mountaineering. He is also joined by John A McIver to take a stroll along the coast between Ness and Tolsta, and discuss Lord Leverhume's influence on the area

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Donald Macsween is back with a special series from his croft in Ness. Cameras follow him from the beginning of the year and into lockdown, getting his thoughts on how the coronavirus has impacted him in his job as a full-time crofter

programme.title: Domhnallaich Na Frainge (The French MacDonalds)
programme.desc: John Morrison tells the story of Neil MacEachen, a Uist Jacobite who saved Bonnie Prince Charlie from capture. He reveals how the islander's son rose to become one of Napoleon's generals and a marshal of France, while MacEachen died in exile and poverty

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Recorded for the Trad Awards 2020, the acclaimed Project Smok perform three tracks

programme.title: Crowdie and Cream

Three-part period drama based on Finlay J MacDonald's trilogy about childhood, family, home and friendship in the Hebrides, starring Iain Macrae

programme.desc: Freaturing acclaimed singer Robert Robertson, Irish supergroup Altan and vocalist Siobhan Wilson. Plus, Ruth Keggan from the Isle of Man and Flavia Coelho from Brazil. Hosted by Cathy MacDonald

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: Lake Titicaca, straddling the border between Peru and Bolivia in the Andes Mountains, is one of South America's largest lakes. Living here are the Uro - an ancient people, described at times as the only decedents of the Incas. They are the people of the floating islands, a very small community of about 2,000 with a community consisting of 50 swimming islands. Every day from the furthest corners, the youngest inhabitants paddle their way in their small boats to school

programme.title: Dhan Uisge

programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day four of the festival, including the 3.40 Commonwealth Cup and 4.20 Coronation Stakes, plus races at 2.30, 3.05, 5.00 and 5.35. With analysis from Jason Weaver, AP McCoy and Kevin Blake, reports by Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Adele Mulrennan, race commentary by Richard Hoiles and Mark Johnson, lifestyle with Mark Heyes and Charlotte Hawkins, and social stable from Oli Bell and Chris Hughes

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: England v Scotland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the Group D fixture, arguably the most anticipated match of the tournament so far, which comes from Wembley Stadium. England defeated Croatia 1-0 in their opening fixture thanks to a second-half goal from Raheem Sterling, while the Scots fell to a 2-0 defeat against Czech Republic. This game reignites the oldest rivalry in international football, and will bring back memories of their famous meeting at the old Wembley 25 years ago during Euro 96. Paul Gascoigne - who was plying his trade for Rangers at the time - was the hero for England that day, scoring a spectacular volley after flicking the ball over the head of Colin Hendry to make it 2-0, just seconds after David Seaman had saved a Gary McAllister penalty. More recently, Harry Kane broke Scottish hearts during a 2018 World Cup qualifier at Hampden Park. Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain gave England the lead late in the second half, but Leigh Griffiths struck two sublime free-kicks in the fi

programme.title: Taken 2 (2012)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Bryan Mills and his ex-wife Lenore are taken hostage in Istanbul by an Albanian gangster, whose son Bryan killed while bringing down a kidnapping ring. He is able to escape with the aid of his daughter, and together they fight to rescue Lenore and wipe out the criminal family. Action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Maggie Grace and Famke Janssen

programme.desc: The raccoons steal Iris's favorite toy but accidentally drop it on the terrace during their escape. Mike picks it up while cleaning, thinking it's Fluffys.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike's plans to hang out with Iris are cruelly dashed when he trots out onto the patio to find her playing with a hunky other dog. Mike is crushed. This means war!

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: On vacation in Rome Gwen takes Team Tennyson on a self-guided tour of the many ancient sites but Ben stumbles upon a Queen Bee heist.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Singer Peter Andre and drag queen the Vivienne join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guest Rob Rinder and chefs Kuba Winkowski and Galton Blackiston. Featuring recipes for sweet and sour chicken, seafood biryani, trout, pork stew, and whisky meringue, and a masterclass in making fudge

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares a collection of simple, rustic recipes that can be put together easily, as well as revealing the workings of the world-famous Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons where he is Chef Patron and which provides much of the fruit and vegetables that go into his dishes. In the first episode, he is joined by Angela Hartnett, who will be cooking cep tagliatelle. Other recipes include meringue with blackcurrant coulis and a heritage salad with beetroot straight from Raymond's gardens

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef begins the Texan leg of his American adventure by visiting a place with more than a hint of Mexican spice, as San Antonio was once a part of Mexico. Amidst the wonders of the city, James tries fresh churros and a traditional barbecoa - Mexican barbecue

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the opening three races on the fifth day of the festival, which take place at 2.30, 3.05 and 3.40. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Kevin Blake, reports by Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Adele Mulrennan, commentary from Richard Hoiles and Mark Johnson, lifestyle with Mark Heyes and Charlotte Hawkins, and social stable with Oli Bell and Chris Hughes

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Portugal v Germany (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the second Group F match for both countries, which takes place at Fußball Arena München. These famous footballing nations have played each other exclusively at major tournaments over the past 21 years, with their last five matches all taking place at either a World Cup or European Championship. Germany hold the advantage in those games, winning four of them, with Portugal's last triumph over Die Mannschaft coming at Euro 2000. However, it is the Portuguese who are the holders and they fared better in their opener, prevailing 3-0 against Hungary, while Joachim Low's men suffered a 1-0 defeat at the hands of France. Match analysis is provided by Gary Neville, Graeme Souness and Patrick Vieira, who won this tournament in 2000 with France. Commentary from Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Wonder Woman (2017)
programme.desc: An Amazon princess who grows up in enchanted realm has her first encounter with the outside world during the First World War. American airman Steve Trevor crash lands on her isolated island home. Determined to bring the chaos sweeping the world to an end, she ventures onto the front line in an attempt to bring down an ancient and powerful evil force that is driving the human race towards destruction. Director Patty Jenkins' blockbusting fantasy adventure, starring Gal Gadot, Chris Pine and David Thewlis

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the latest fixtures, featuring Spain v Poland at Estadio La Cartuja in Group E, and Hungary v France at Puskas Arena and Portugal v Germany Fußball Arena München in Group F, as the teams involved all played their second matches of the tournament. Seema Jaswal presents, with analysis from Emma Hayes and Robert Earnshaw

programme.title: Rush Hour (1998)
programme.desc: A Hong Kong detective is sent to Los Angeles to investigate a kidnapping, but the local authorities worry that his presence in America might spark an international incident, and assign a loud-mouthed and troublesome cop to keep an eye on him. However, the new partnership proves an explosive combination. Comedy adventure, starring Jackie Chan, Chris Tucker, Ken Leung, Tom Wilkinson and Chris Penn

programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Hrvy and Big Zuu join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Woking, Surrey, to transform the garden of John Phillips, a retired serviceman who lost his left arm when a bomb exploded on board HMS Antelope in the Falkland Islands in 1982. The experts plant beds of brightly coloured flowers, a vegetable patch designed to be easily accessible and build a corner seating area, before completing their work with a piece of Falklands rock donated by John's former commanding officer

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Banana, Mouse, Frog, Taco and Kitty perform for guest judge Gabriel Iglesias and the rest of the panell before one of the singers is unmasked and sent home. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Kitty, Taco, Banana and Frog perform for guest judge T-Pain and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Junior (1994)
programme.desc: A fertility expert pioneers a treatment enabling men to become pregnant and carry babies to full term. When the authorities cancel funding for the research, he decides to test it on himself, but gives little thought to how difficult it will be to keep it a secret. The situation is further complicated when he starts to fall for the woman whose child he is carrying. Comedy, with Arnold Schwarzenegger, Danny DeVito, Emma Thompson and Frank Langella

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Italy v Wales (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group A fixture for both countries, which takes place at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Roberto Mancini's Azzurri became the first side to qualify for the knockout phase on Wednesday evening with their second consecutive 3-0 victory, this time against Switzerland. A draw for Wales will be enough to guarantee their place in the last 16, and victory would ensure they finish top of the group ahead of the Italians. Robert Page will be hoping he can inspire his players to produce a performance reminiscent of October 2002, when Wales famously defeated an Italian side containing the likes of Gianluigi Buffon, Fabio Cannavaro, Andrea Pirlo and Alessandro Del Piero 2-1 in front of 70,000 fans in Cardiff. Page will surely have been calling on the knowledge of midfielder Aaron Ramsey, who plies his trade for Juventus, ahead of this game, while talismanic forward Gareth Bale will also be familiar with the Italian style of play

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Noah makes a discovery. Diane confides in Gabby. Vic drops a bombshell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: To celebrate 70 years in the film industry, the actress sits down with Piers for a special in which she covers everything from her time as a young starlet appearing alongside Diana Dors and Bette Davis to her reign as Dynasty legend Alexis Carrington. She tells Piers what she really thought of her famous co-stars and ex-husbands, lifts the lid on her tumultuous affair with Warren Beatty and relives the day she discovered her sister Jackie was terminally ill

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: Previously unseen footage from the actress's travelogue programmes, beginning with a look at her journey from Japan to Siberia. The trip begins on the frozen Japanese sea of Okhotsk, and goes across China and Mongolia before arriving at Lake Baikal in Siberia, where Joanna meets some amazing seals

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Eight celebrities prepare to embark on a life changing journey to explore their family history using DNA technology and genealogy. This time, Jamie Redknapp and Freddie Flintoff travel around the country to discover each other's family histories. Proud Lancastrian Freddie is anxious that his DNA may lead him to Yorkshire, and there is a surprise in store when the pair arrive the Wakefield Mining Museum. Later, Jamie's family journey takes him to London's East End, where he uncovers a notorious mystery that has remained unsolved for generations

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: ITV Racing Live

With analysis from Jason Weaver, AP McCoy and Kevin Blake, reports by Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Adele Mulrennan, race commentary by Richard Hoiles and Mark Johnson, lifestyle with Mark Heyes and Charlotte Hawkins, and social stable from Oli Bell and Chris Hughes

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: England v Scotland (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the Group D fixture, arguably the most anticipated match of the tournament so far, which comes from Wembley Stadium. England defeated Croatia 1-0 in their opening fixture thanks to a second-half goal from Raheem Sterling, while the Scots fell to a 2-0 defeat against Czech Republic. This game reignites the oldest rivalry in international football, and will bring back memories of their famous meeting at the old Wembley 25 years ago during Euro 96. Paul Gascoigne - who was plying his trade for Rangers at the time - was the hero for England that day, scoring a spectacular volley after flicking the ball over the head of Colin Hendry to make it 2-0, just seconds after David Seaman had saved a Gary McAllister penalty. More recently, Harry Kane broke Scottish hearts during a 2018 World Cup qualifier at Hampden Park. Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain gave England the lead late in the second half, but Leigh Griffiths struck two sublime free-kicks in the fi

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Taken 2 (2012)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Bryan Mills and his ex-wife Lenore are taken hostage in Istanbul by an Albanian gangster, whose son Bryan killed while bringing down a kidnapping ring. He is able to escape with the aid of his daughter, and together they fight to rescue Lenore and wipe out the criminal family. Action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Maggie Grace and Famke Janssen

programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the village prepares for the annual flower show, Isobel finds herself in conflict with Violet, and Mary is introduced to a potential suitor, but it would seem she only has eyes for Matthew. Anna realises who is to blame when a valuable snuff box goes missing, and Mrs Patmore tearfully reveals her fears to Carson that her career may be coming to an end. Meanwhile, O'Brien engineers a way for Edith to take revenge on Mary. Drama, starring Maggie Smith and Robert Bathurst

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor becomes convinced something is wrong with him following a conversation with his mother, and Louisa's attempts at getting him to open up about the loss of his father prove futile. Mrs Tishell goes to the surgery for advice regarding a pain in her jaw, only to find herself falling in love with Martin all over again. Elsewhere, Al receives a visit from two military policemen who are trying to track down Mike Pruddy

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa decides to leave for Spain with baby James and says an awkward goodbye to Martin, but when he finds the scan of her brain, he spots a malformation and realises she could suffer a haemorrhage if she gets on the plane. Will the doctor make it to the airport in time? Meanwhile, Margaret reveals the real reason she has come to Portwenn, and disaster strikes at Bert and Jennifer's engagement party when Caroline receives an electric shock

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Racial tensions erupt in Hastings when a black GI is suspected of murdering a local woman. The victim, an ex-lover of the suspect and the mother of his baby, was staying at a guest house where Samantha is assistant manager, and had been disowned by her family because of her mixed-race child. As Foyle investigates, he is not convinced the soldier could be capable of such a crime, and soon comes up against the might of the US Army as he tries to crack the case. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Obi Abili and Charlotte Riley

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The second series of the drama about the early years of Queen Victoria's reign begins after the birth of her first child. She resumes her royal duties sooner than expected, and quickly becomes convinced that her husband and the prime minister are concealing troubling news about the conflict in Afghanistan, and turns to the Duke of Wellington for answers. Victoria also faces problems at home, as Albert's family descend on the palace for the christening with plans for the future. Diana Rigg joins the cast as the queen's new confidante, the formidable Duchess of Buccleuch

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: The queen suspects that her husband is attracted to another woman, and discovers that she is pregnant with a second child - leaving her concerned that motherhood will dominate her life and reign, at the expense of any other possibilities. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings is staying with friends when wealthy heiress Mrs Inglethorp is murdered. He calls in Hercule Poirot to help him solve the baffling crime, which has the whole household under suspicion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth, with Hugh Fraser, Pauline Moran, Anthony Calf and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Bentham DeQuetteville falls to his death from a roof after seeing a headless horseman, his aristocratic family seem more concerned about their forthcoming Civil War re-enactment than the incident. As Barnaby and Jones try to discover the truth behind the ghostly figure, their investigation uncovers shocking secrets about the DeQuettevilles. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Two couples disappear in the tiny village of Monks Barton amid rumours of witchcraft and haunted woods. As Barnaby and Jones investigate, psychic Cyrus LeVanu clashes with cleric Wallace Stone over their different methods of 'healing' the woods, while other locals seem to be caught up in a stolen antiques racket. When a body is unearthed and the death toll rises, the detectives race to catch the killer by finding a connection between the missing couples and the thefts. Anton Lesser guest stars, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: Drama about England and West Ham star Bobby Moore's life with his childhood sweetheart Tina Dean, detailing how the pair rose to the dizzy heights of superstardom. In the opening instalment, the 19-year-old insurance office typist agrees to marry the footballer, little knowing that their marriage will be defined by history. Their lives change markedly when the talented player becomes England captain aged just 23. However, when he is diagnosed with cancer Tina proves Bobby's rock as he fights his way back to fitness in a bid to compete in the World Cup of 1966. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: West Ham player Bobby leads England to World Cup glory at Wembley and he and Tina are reunited amidst the raucous celebrations. Fame and fortune follow as the couple become icons of their time, and when baby Dean arrives Tina thinks she has it all. The price of celebrity soon becomes apparent when Tina is besieged after Bobby is arrested in Colombia accused of theft, and although he goes on to play well at the 1970 Mexico World Cup, they return home to a kidnap threat and a wedge increasingly being driven between them. Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan star

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid

programme.desc: David Suchet takes on his final case as the Belgian sleuth in the last-ever episode of the long-running drama. Crippled with arthritis, Poirot is now using a wheelchair and has a life-threatening heart condition, but remains as sharp as ever. Calling on old friend Captain Hastings for assistance, he returns to Styles, where the pair first met 30 years earlier on a murder investigation. The detective is convinced the country house is once again harbouring a killer, and as guests fall victim to a mysterious attacker, he must summon the last of his strength to battle his ultimate nemesis. With Hugh Fraser, Anne Reid, Philip Glenister, Helen Baxendale, Matthew McNulty, Anna Madeley and Shaun Dingwall

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barrister is killed with a lethal dose of morphine and suspicion falls on ex-convict Grady Felton, who has recently returned to the village where he committed murder years earlier. However, he has a strong alibi and Barnaby thinks he must be working with an accomplice - until Grady himself is targeted in an arson attack. Detective drama, guest starring Sharon Duce, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: As darkness covers Midsomer Stanton during a total eclipse of the sun, Jeremy Harper is killed by a blow to the head with a meteorite. The victim was a leading light in the village - an amateur astronomer who was passionate about preserving Moonstone Ridge, a Bronze Age heritage site housing a 1930s observatory. Barnaby and Jones are called in to investigate, and the duo discover that intrigue, sexual tension and academic rivalry are rife among the local stargazing community. Murder mystery, guest starring Maureen Lipman and Kenny Ireland, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called to the University of Northumberland when Jamie Marshall, a promising young journalism student, falls to his death from the top of a disused faculty building. With no eyewitnesses and no immediate leads, Vera turns to friends and family to glean all she can about Jamie, and to discover who might have wanted him dead. The student's innocent facade is revealed to have concealed simmering resentment and anger which he expressed on the internet - and which may have made him some deadly enemies

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. Before long a second death occurs, and it becomes apparent that both victims shared a secret - and that someone is out for revenge. Starring Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Anna Massey, Clare Holman and Sian Thomas

programme.title: Tina & Bobby
programme.desc: The duo try to escape their marriage troubles by investing in their future, but a disastrous business venture mires them in debt just as Bobby's playing career starts to wane. Bobby winds up managing a lowly non-league side, and Tina dismisses rumours he's having an affair - until her worst fears are confirmed. When he confesses to loving the woman involved, her world collapses along with her marriage and she finds she must start her life afresh. Drama, starring Lorne MacFadyen and Michelle Keegan

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The discovery of a body at a ramshackle farm alerts Wycliffe to the fact that a murderer is at large in a sleepy Cornish village - and when two locals disappear, he realises it will take all his deductive powers to solve the mystery. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Having spent the night in the ginnel, Nina staggers home, lying to Roy about where she has been. Aled's consultant confirms he is an ideal candidate for a cochlear implant operation, while a troubled Mary tells Bernie what she heard Dev saying to Steve. When Evelyn calls to pick up the girls, she's gobsmacked by Tyrone's new look, and Billy gets a call from Summer's school

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
